steps.title: Identify the grammatical role needed
steps.content: The sentence is: “She typically ___ them from sumac and cedar bark.” The blank is the only verb in this clause, so it must be a finite (conjugated) verb that agrees with the singular subject “She” and expresses habitual action signaled by “typically”.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — weaving: Present participle (non-finite form). Violates finite verb requirement — “She typically weaving them” lacks a main verb and creates a sentence fragment. ✗
  • Choice B — to weave: Infinitive (non-finite form). Violates finite verb requirement — an infinitive cannot serve as the predicate of a standalone clause. ✗
  • Choice C — weaves: Third-person singular present tense (finite). Matches singular subject “She” and correctly expresses habitual action with “typically”. ✓
  • Choice D — to be weaving: Progressive infinitive (non-finite form). Violates finite verb requirement — same problem as B; cannot serve as the main predicate. ✗
C is correct.
